location.office_time_required: Monday - Thursday in-office (four days a week in the office, Fridays from home)

seniority.level: Mid to Senior IC (PM II through Senior PM, depending on experience)
level_signals: 4–10+ years of experience as a Product Manager (leveling will vary based on experience and scope)
Comp range $150,000–$244,000 base, consistent with mid-to-senior IC range at a late-stage fintech

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 4–10+ years of experience as a Product Manager (leveling will vary based on experience and scope)
Experience working on complex product problems involving risk, operations, platforms, or customer-facing systems
Strong product judgment and the ability to navigate ambiguity and make principled tradeoffs
A strategic mindset paired with the ability to translate vision into practical execution
Proven ability to influence and align cross-functional stakeholders across technical and non-technical teams
Strong analytical skills, with experience using data to inform decisions and measure impact
Customer empathy, with the ability to deeply understand user needs and translate them into product solutions
Comfort operating in fast-moving, high-stakes environments with evolving constraints
Passion for Chime's mission of helping members lead healthier financial lives

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Enrollment & KYC: Build onboarding and identity verification systems that enable fast, low-friction access for legitimate members while preventing fraud such as synthetic identity attacks.
Member Support Experience - Human Agents: Define the tools, workflows, and systems that empower agents to resolve complex member issues with speed, accuracy, and empathy at scale

Member Support Experience - Automation and AI Agents: Develop platforms and automation systems—including AI-driven capabilities—that scale support and back office operations and improve both efficiency and member experience

Disputes: Transform member experiences and design intelligent systems that help members navigate and resolve transaction disputes, balancing member trust, fraud risk, loss exposure, and operational efficiency
Build and evolve systems that leverage data, experimentation, and (where applicable) machine learning and AI to improve outcomes

scope.surface: Trust & Safety product domains: Enrollment & KYC, Member Support (Human Agents and Automation/AI), Disputes
primary_metrics_inferred: Operational efficiency (support automation, dispute resolution throughput)
Member onboarding conversion and KYC pass rates
core_responsibilities: Define and drive product strategy and roadmap within Trust & Safety, balancing customer experience, risk mitigation, and operational efficiency
Own ambiguous, high-impact problem spaces and make thoughtful tradeoffs across technology, operations, compliance, and business goals
Make high-stakes tradeoffs where decisions directly impact fraud loss, member trust, and regulatory compliance
Partner deeply with cross-functional teams—including Engineering, Data Science, Design, Operations, Risk, and Compliance—to deliver scalable, reliable systems
Build and evolve systems that leverage data, experimentation, and (where applicable) machine learning and AI to improve outcomes
Define clear metrics and success frameworks, using data to drive continuous improvement across member experience, risk outcomes, and operational performance
Influence cross-functional strategy and align stakeholders across diverse priorities and constraints
Drive execution in mission-critical environments where reliability, security, and compliance are non-negotiable
Contribute to the evolution of Trust & Safety and Operations at Chime by identifying new opportunities, shaping long-term strategy, and raising the bar for product thinking

green_flags: Multiple sub-domain specializations offered (KYC, support, disputes) — role can align to candidate strengths
Clear articulation of cross-functional partners and problem spaces
High-impact, mission-critical work with tangible outcomes (fraud loss, member trust, regulatory compliance)
Bonus and equity package mentioned in addition to base salary
Strong benefits package including up to 24 weeks paid parental leave and family planning support
red_flags: Hybrid requirement is four days per week in San Francisco office (Mon–Thu), one of the more demanding in-office schedules in the industry — eliminates most non-SF candidates
Wide comp range ($150K–$244K) spans multiple seniority levels, making it unclear which level is actually being filled
JD covers multiple distinct roles under one posting, which may indicate loose scope definition or unclear hiring intent for any single PM
Visa sponsorship not addressed anywhere in the JD

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and years range; JD explicitly states 'leveling will vary')
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated as metrics in JD)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description as B2C fintech/neobank)
location.visa_sponsorship (not mentioned anywhere in JD; marked unclear)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred as false from 'This role is in-office in San Francisco Monday - Thursday')
ambiguities: The JD is a multi-role posting covering at least four distinct product domains (Enrollment & KYC, Member Support Human Agents, Member Support Automation/AI, Disputes). It is unclear whether a single candidate is expected to cover all areas or will be placed in one. Nice-to-haves were coded based on the domain-specific bullets since they describe preferred specializations rather than universal requirements.

Years of experience range '4–10+' is unusually wide and the JD explicitly notes 'leveling will vary based on experience and scope,' meaning the actual level and requirements are assessed per candidate during the process.
Comp range $150,000–$244,000 spans what is typically PM II through Staff PM at a late-stage fintech, reinforcing level ambiguity.
